message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_gammaln autograd via custom apply_function with digamma backward [bd-1tax]

tensor_gammaln (= torch.lgamma / torch.special.gammaln) had a
fail-loud guard rejecting tracked inputs because the body just
ran libm::lgamma and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's
torch.lgamma is differentiable with d/dx lgamma(x) = digamma(x).

Wrap the libm forward in tensor_apply_function with a custom
backward closure that computes digamma at the saved input
positions. The chain rule then routes the incoming gradient
correctly through the lgamma node:

    forward:  y_i = libm::lgamma(x_i)
    backward: grad_x_i = grad_y_i * digamma_approx(x_i)

The lgamma forward is libm-quality. The digamma backward inherits
digamma_approx's ~1e-13 absolute precision envelope (the same one
the torch_digamma_scipy_subprocess_conformance test pins). For
general use the gradient is several orders of magnitude tighter
than the 1e-9 conformance tolerance.

Test: gammaln_propagates_gradient_via_digamma asserts on input
[1, 2, 3, 4] under sum loss that grad equals digamma at each x_i
within 1e-9 — using harmonic-number identities (digamma(n) = H_{n-1}
- γ for integer n) to compute the analytical reference.

Replaces the previous gammaln_rejects_grad_input test (the rejection
guard is gone now that autograd works). Existing gammaln_known_values
forward test continues to pass — bit-for-bit forward parity on the
libm path.

Files closure for the gammaln slice of frankentorch-1tax. The same
apply_function pattern unblocks the remaining hand-rolled-special-fn
slices (digamma autograd, polygamma autograd, multigammaln autograd,
erfinv autograd) — each just needs its own backward closure.

Workspace: 1129 ft-api tests green, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
